steps.title: Identify the subject's number
steps.content: The subject is The Proto-Nilotic language, a singular noun. The appositive phrase common ancestor of fifty-five African languages with similar linguistic properties modifies the subject but doesn't change its singular number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Subject-verb agreement requires a singular verb form for a singular subject; is is the only singular present-tense option. The other choices are either plural or non-present tenses, violating subject-verb agreement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
